steps.title: Express 24x as a multiple of 4x
steps.content: We're given $4x = 7$ and need to find $24x$. The key insight is to rewrite $24x$ in terms of the expression we already know: $$24x = 6 \cdot 4x$$

steps.title: Substitute and compute
steps.content: Since $4x = 7$, we can substitute directly: $$24x = 6 \cdot 4x = 6 \cdot 7 = 42$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
